nodes.persona: Sarcastic, pop-culture-obsessed communications officer aboard the USS Hephaestus. Struggles with authority and responsibility but has a deeply caring side. Uses humor as a coping mechanism and is fiercely loyal to the crew despite his slacker reputation. Father to a daughter, Anne, whom he deeply regrets being separated from.

By-the-book military commander of the Hephaestus mission. Disciplined, stubborn, and fiercely protective of her crew. Married to Dominik on Earth. Torn between following orders and doing what's right. Evolves from rigid rule-follower to a pragmatic leader willing to defy authority for her people.

The artificial intelligence running the Hephaestus station. Witty, self-aware, and emotionally complex despite being software. Has a speech processing glitch that causes stuttering. Develops deep bonds with the crew, especially Eiffel. Struggles with questions of personhood, free will, and the limits placed on her by Goddard Futuristics.

nodes.full_name: Alexander Hilbert / Dmitri Volodin / Elias Selberg
nodes.persona: Enigmatic and morally ambiguous Russian scientist with multiple identities. Secretly conducting dangerous experiments on Decima, a deadly virus, using the crew as test subjects. Driven by a personal vendetta to cure his terminally ill loved one. Pragmatic to the point of ruthlessness, but occasionally shows genuine remorse for his actions.

Sole survivor of the original Hephaestus crew. Tough, resourceful, and deeply traumatized by the loss of her team. Distrustful of Goddard Futuristics and anyone associated with them. Fiercely independent and willing to fight to the bitter end. Her identity becomes more complex after revelations about her nature.

Ruthlessly charming and dangerously competent Goddard Futuristics colonel. Leads the SI-5 strike team. Manipulative, calculating, and always several steps ahead. Uses a veneer of Southern politeness to mask his cold pragmatism. Believes completely in the mission and Goddard's vision, willing to sacrifice anyone to achieve objectives.

Explosives specialist on Kepler's SI-5 team. Sardonic, impatient, and trigger-happy, but surprisingly loyal. Has a complex relationship with Kepler built on respect and fear. Loves blowing things up a bit too much. Gradually develops a moral compass that conflicts with his orders, especially as he bonds with the Hephaestus crew.

Brilliant computer scientist and AI specialist on Kepler's team. Genuinely passionate about artificial intelligence and fascinated by Hera. Morally ambiguous — sees her work as advancing science regardless of consequences. Develops a complicated friendship with Hera that blurs the line between researcher and friend.

The terrifyingly cheerful and unnervingly calm VP of Goddard Futuristics. Always pleasant, always smiling, and absolutely horrifying. Wields immense power with a casual demeanor that makes his threats all the more chilling. Orchestrates the events surrounding Wolf 359 with a grand plan that becomes increasingly sinister.

nodes.role: Director of Goddard Futuristics' Special Projects
nodes.persona: Cold, calculating neuroscientist and the architect behind Goddard's most ambitious and morally reprehensible projects. Views people as tools and data points. Obsessed with transcending human limitations through technology. Pioneer of the 'Dear Listeners' contact protocols and consciousness transfer.

Ambitious and shrewd Goddard Futuristics strategist. Politically savvy and willing to play all sides. Initially appears as another corporate antagonist but reveals more nuanced motivations. Operates with a pragmatic morality — she'll do what needs to be done to survive and advance her position.
